tables.title: Pediatric Assessment Triangle — Components and Findings
rows: Active movement, engaged, consolable, strong cry
Limp, inconsolable, weak/absent cry, no eye contact
Normal rate and rhythm, clear breath sounds, no retractions
Retractions, nasal flaring, grunting, stridor, wheezing, accessory muscle use
Pink skin, moist mucous membranes, cap refill < 3 sec
Pallor, cyanosis, mottling, delayed cap refill, dry mucous membranes

tables.title: Three Categories of Shock in Children
rows: Flat neck veins, tachycardia, weak pulses, cool extremities, delayed cap refill
Warm/flushed initially (warm shock), then cold/mottled (cold shock); bounding pulses early
Myocarditis, structural heart disease, cardiac tamponade, dysrhythmia
JVD, gallop rhythm, hepatomegaly, pulmonary edema, weak pulses

tables.title: Respiratory Distress Categories in PALS
rows: Epinephrine (IM or nebulized), dexamethasone, prepare for airway
Below the vocal cords, at the bronchi
PE, congenital heart disease with shunting
